mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, increasing present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a hit rate above 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the industrial production index during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth signals lose predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when CPIAUCSL monthly changes exceed 0.5%, or forward returns are flat/negative in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% year-over-year growth triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Markets fail to sell off following CPIAUCSL spikes > 0.4% MoM, with 5-day forward returns remaining positive in > 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Alignment between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales trends confirms robust aggregate demand momentum, reducing recession probability and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where payroll growth decelerates while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously signals labor market fragmentation and weakening economic momentum, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 100k and UNRATE rises
failure_condition: The specified labor divergence condition fails to predict negative returns, with 20-day forward returns remaining positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 4-year sample.
